markdown_heading: AUTH-101 — Password reset with rate limiting
change_summary: Added password-reset service with single-use, time-bound tokens and per-account rate limiting; all issuance/consumption audited.
critical_invariants: Reset tokens are single-use and expire; a consumed or expired token is always rejected.
Reset requests never reveal whether an account exists (no enumeration).
Every token issuance and consumption is written to the audit log with actor and timestamp.
requirement_closure: Ticket ask: Add password reset with per-account rate limiting and audited token issuance (FR-1.1).

Implemented: passwordReset service with single-use time-bound tokens, per-account rate limit, and audit records on issue/consume; constant-time response to avoid enumeration; unit tests cover expiry, reuse, rate limit, and enumeration.
repo_gates: cd backend && npm run build [result=pass; attribution=clean] - tsc clean
cd backend && npm test -- passwordReset [result=pass; attribution=clean] - 11 passed

self_review_cycles.findings: single-use enforced via consumed flag; expiry checked before use; tests assert both
constant-time response regardless of account existence; rate limit keyed per account_id; enumeration + limit tests pass
issue/consume both audited; structured log fields; no hot-path N+1
every FR-1.1 clause mapped to code + test; closeout complete
